title: Plutarchia pubiflora
taxonomy: Plutarchia pubiflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae, commonly known as the gentian family. It is native to Mexico and belongs to the genus Plutarchia, which contains several species of plants. The classification of Plutarchia pubiflora is based on its unique characteristics, such as its flowers and leaves. The plant is further classified as a member of the order Gentianales, which includes other plants like gentians and oleanders. Plutarchia pubiflora is an important part of the plant kingdom, with its classification providing valuable information about its evolution and relationships to other plants.
uses: Plutarchia pubiflora is a plant species with limited information available on its medicinal uses. However, some studies suggest that it may have potential as a source of bioactive compounds.
